summary: labeler.test.ts has good coverage of core assignLabels functionality (empty files, glob matching, multiple labels, priority/group conflict resolution, deduplication, sorting, error validation). workflow.test.ts validates workflow structure, triggers, and steps; runs actionlint; and performs act end-to-end testing. However, critical gaps: (1) Glob pattern edge cases untested—the globToRegex function supports **, *, ?, and bracket classes, but tests lack cases for complex patterns like src/**/test/**/*.ts, bracket classes [abc], or ** without slash; (2) CLI entrypoint completely untested—JSON parsing, stdin reading, error messages, and exit codes are not exercised; (3) Fixture files (fixtures/case1.json, case2.json, case3.json) are referenced in workflow.test.ts but not created or shown in test code, creating external dependencies that make tests harder to understand and maintain; (4) Missing edge cases: what if priority is explicitly 0, group without priority, or null/undefined rule properties. Test design couples the workflow tests tightly to existence of fixture files without clear fixture creation/documentation. Would catch regressions in core assignment logic and workflow structure, but would miss glob-matching bugs, CLI issues, and edge-case behavior.
